title: 10 best herbal essential oils for lymph node swelling
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for lymph node swelling are natural remedies that help alleviate swelling and inflammation of the lymph nodes. They work by stimulating the lymphatic system, reducing pain and promoting healing. The best herbal essential oils for this condition include Echinacea purpurea, Echinacea angustifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Melaleuca alternifolia, Silybum marianum, Ginkgo biloba, Calendula officinalis, Thuja occidentalis, Zingiber officinale, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, lymph node swelling can lead to chronic fatigue, immune system suppression, and even organ damage, significantly impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium essential oils helps with lymph node swelling because of its unique ability to stimulate lymphatic circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote detoxification. The oil's citral content has been shown to increase the production of lymphatic enzymes, which helps to break down and eliminate toxins from the body. Additionally, geranium essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with lymph node enlargement, making it a natural and effective remedy for promoting healthy lymphatic function.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Geranium essential oil's antioxidant property helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to lymph node swelling by preventing damage to surrounding tissues.

Anti-inflammatory property: Geranium essential oil's anti-inflammatory property reduces swelling and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory hormones that can exacerbate lymph node swelling.

Stimulatory property: Geranium essential oil's stimulatory property helps to stimulate the lymphatic system, improving its function and promoting the removal of toxins and waste products that can accumulate in swollen lymph nodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Geranium roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce lymph node swelling by promoting healthy immune function.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of geranium plants contain natural diuretics that help alleviate fluid retention and swelling in the lymph nodes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves in the early morning for optimal oil content and quality.
Rinse the harvested leaves gently with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steam distill the washed leaves at 100°c for 4 hours using a clevenger apparatus to extract essential oil.
Monitor the distillation process carefully and collect the extracted oil as it is separated from water using a hydrometer.
Filter the collected oil through a cotton cloth or paper filter to remove any impurities or sediment.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn or early morning when oil content is highest.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation for 7-10 days to reduce moisture levels below 10%.
Use a steam distillation apparatus with 0.5 kg of dried melissa leaves and 1 l of distilled water at 100°c for 30 minutes.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter or paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, keeping away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from a clean environment ensuring proper handling.
Chop the harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves into smaller pieces for efficient steam distillation.
Combine 100g of chopped eucalyptus globulus leaves with 500ml of distilled water in a stainless steel pot.
Steam distill the mixture for 3 hours at atmospheric pressure and moderate heat to extract essential oil.
Separate the extracted essential oil from the distillate using a separation funnel and store in a dark glass bottle.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested material for at least 14 days in a warm well-ventilated area.
Steam distill the dried herbs using 100 milliliters of water for every 500 milligrams of plant material.
Collect the essential oil after 30 minutes of distillation and filter it through a coffee filter paper.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in full bloom at dawn or late afternoon for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in small batches using a dehydrator on low heat for 3-5 hours or air-drying for several days.
Combine dried flowers with carrier oil in a 1:4 ratio and store the mixture in an airtight container for 14-21 days to allow infusion.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids, and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.
Use the resulting essential oil undiluted or mixed with a carrier oil in a 1:10 ratio, applying topically 2-3 times daily to affected areas or inhaling through steam inhalation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thuja occidentalis essential oils helps with lymph node swelling because of its natural antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like thujone and bornyl acetate, which have been traditionally used to promote lymphatic drainage and reduce swelling. By stimulating lymphatic circulation, Thuja occidentalis essential oils can help to eliminate toxins and waste from the body, thereby alleviating lymph node swelling. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to support immune function and promote overall health.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate lymph node inflammation.
Bornyl acetate: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent and an antioxidant, which together help to reduce swelling and promote healing in lymph nodes.
Cedrol: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to reduce lymph node swelling and fight off underlying infections that may be causing the swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and promote lymphatic drainage.
Barks: They are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing swelling and inflammation.

Buds: They are used due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and promote lymphatic drainage, as well as their ability to stimulate the lymphatic system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thuja occidentalis leaves and twigs from mature plants with 50 to 70 percent essential oil content.
Steam distill the harvested plant material in a 2:1 ratio of plant material to distilled water for 4 hours.
Collect the distilled water and plant oil mixture and separate the essential oil from water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 10-micron filter paper to remove any remaining plant particles and debris.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass container away from direct sunlight and heat sources for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with lymph node swelling because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen lymph nodes. The essential oil's active compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which help to combat oxidative stress and promote healing in the affected area. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Zingiber officinale essential oils may help to alleviate lymph node swelling and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with lymph node inflammation.
Shogaol: This constituent has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lymphatic system.
Zingiberene: It has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce bacterial infections and inflammation in the lymph nodes, promoting healing and reducing swelling.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Zingiber officinale are commonly used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce lymph node swelling.

Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make essential oils, which contain volatile oils that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate lymph node swelling.
Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale are used to make essential oils, which contain compounds that have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties and may help reduce swelling in lymph nodes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rhizomes of zingiber officinale and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steam distill the rhizomes at a temperature of 100-110 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled steam and condense it into a liquid using a condenser coil.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separating funnel and let it sit for 2-3 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a glass wool filter to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with lymph node swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and promote the healing process. The oil's antiseptic and antimicrobial properties can also help prevent infection and promote the removal of toxins that may be causing the swelling. Additionally, Lavandula angustifolia's ability to relax the body and mind can help reduce stress, which is often a contributing factor to lymph node swelling. This makes it a promising natural remedy for alleviating lymph node swelling.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with lymph node swelling.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing swelling and promoting lymphatic drainage.
Limonene: A terpene that has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, potentially helping to alleviate lymph node swelling and promote overall lymphatic health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lavender leaves help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation.
Flowers: The flowers of Lavandula angustifolia contain linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that aid in reducing lymph node swelling.
Stems: The stems of lavender contain sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature lavandula angustifolia flowers in full bloom early morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the freshly harvested flowers in a single layer for 7-10 days in a warm room.
Steam distill 200 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 500 grams of water at 100 degrees celsius.
Collect the distilled essential oil from the steam distillation apparatus and separate from the water.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter and bottle in a dark glass container.
